title: unsafe Send impl has correct and complete SAFETY comment
description: Device explicitly implements Send with a SAFETY comment ('WOLFTPM2_DEV holds no thread-local state and no raw pointers shared across threads. The Box gives it a stable address.'). Intentionally not implementing Sync is the correct choice for a non-thread-safe C library. A future contributor might suggest deriving or auto-implementing Sync to allow shared access; this should be rejected — wolfTPM2_* functions are not thread-safe and concurrent calls on the same device without external locking would corrupt the cmdBuf internal state.
